description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white, featuring a blue stripe along the windows and the iconic Pan American World Airways (Pan Am) branding. The aircraft has four underwing jet engines, two on each wing, and a swept low-wing configuration that extends broadly from the fuselage. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer adorned with the Pan Am globe logo and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a series of cockpit windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is partially visible, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit, all of which appear to be retractable. The overall design is sleek and aerodynamic, typical of mid-20th-century long-range passenger jets.
description: A large, white commercial jet airliner with blue accents, four underwing engines, and Pan Am branding. Features a swept low-wing design, conventional tail, and forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, four underwing engines, Pan Am branding, and the vertical stabilizer with the logo.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, forward fuselage, and the full wingspan with all four engines visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage, engines, and tail details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the trailing edges of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
